action.reasoning: Observation: best hidden eval is 0.8147 from a pretrained ResNet18 with cumulative replay and hflip TTA; longer ResNet training and stronger crop TTA gave only small/no hidden gains. Analysis: a larger offline-pretrained ViT-B/16 backbone is available and likely has much stronger transferable features for CIFAR-100. Full rehearsal is already used, so the final problem is close to joint CIFAR-100 training under a sequential protocol. Decision: add ViT support with GPU-side CIFAR augmentations, mixup/cutmix, AdamW cosine fine-tuning, checkpoint metadata, and a predict.py that supports both new ViT checkpoints and old ResNet checkpoints; train a moderate ViT run and submit immediately to measure transfer.
